untranslatable_reason: Signal requires manual measurement of consolidation width (W in columns) and base formation lowest price (P_L), which are pattern-recognition parameters that cannot be expressed using the allowed technical indicators. The core formula H_U = P_L + (W × R) requires identifying specific price bar formations and counting their width—this is a visual/structural analysis task rather than a quantitative indicator-based rule. Operators like 'crosses_above' cannot express the reversal count multiplication. Recommend implementing as a custom pattern detection function outside the standard indicator framework.
translation_notes: KAUFMAN method is a classic Point & Figure-based pattern recognition technique that measures consolidation width and applies it to project upside targets. The entry condition requires identification of a base formation in ranging market. Exit condition marked AUTHOR_SILENT interpreted as breakout above upper Bollinger Band (reversal signal). Parameters W and R require manual pattern measurement and cannot be derived from standard OHLCV indicators alone.
